ogTitle: Laurel Hill vs Lavadia: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Laurel Hill and Lavadia.
heading: Laurel Hill vs Lavadia — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Laurel Hill, New South Wales and Lavadia, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lavadia has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Laurel Hill (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Laurel Hill or Lavadia safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Laurel Hill has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Lavadia scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Laurel Hill scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Lavadia scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Laurel Hill or Lavadia?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lavadia has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
